destinations.evidence: Both Phase 1 and delivery settlement builders resolve the TEE executor through TEEServiceRegistry, reject zero/missing payment addresses, and encode/payment fields with executor.payment_address.

equivalence_argument: Settlement construction uses the contract job TEE address only as a registry lookup key and writes the resolved payment address into settlement calldata and TxAsyncSettlement fields.
notes: Mapped in the port payload builder.
rationale: The commit body calls this out as a fix for Phase 2 settlements using TEE address and causing verifier payment-address mismatches. The payload builder now has lookup_payment_address and uses payment_address in both Phase 1 and Phase 2 encoded settlement parameters. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Phase 1 and Phase 2 async settlement transaction creation must resolve the executor TEE address from the commitment metadata through TEEServiceRegistry and encode the resolved payment address in the settlement transaction; if lookup fails or the TEE is unregistered, settlement creation must return an error instead of falling back to the TEE address.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build Phase 1 and Phase 2 settlement transactions from metadata whose commitment executor_address is TEE and whose registry record maps TEE to payment address P. Decode the settlement transaction input and assert executor_address is P. Repeat with no registry record and assert settlement creation returns an error and does not create a transaction using TEE.
summary: Settlement builders pay the registry payment address.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build Phase 1 and Phase 2 settlements for a TEE mapped to payment address P and assert decoded executor_address is P; remove the registry record and assert construction errors.
title: Settlements pay registry payment address
